title: OpenClaw memory status should not render missing file and chunk counts as placeholders

source.license_note: Localized ACBench reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62855.

task.summary: Repair memory status rendering so plugin-backed providers without counts do not print placeholder file and chunk values.
task.instructions: Investigate why plugin-backed memory status lines print missing counts as visible placeholders, repair the rendering path, and preserve the existing rendering when counts are present.
task.acceptance_notes: Alternative fixes are allowed as long as missing counts no longer appear as placeholders.

visible_context.issue_text: Healthy memory plugins can render status lines like `undefined files · undefined chunks` even when the plugin is otherwise ready.
error_logs: Memory │ plugin memory-lancedb-pro · vector ready · fts ready · None files · None chunks
reproduction_steps: Run the unit tests from the repository root.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models the status-line rendering path for plugin-backed memory providers.

code_fault.description: The status renderer prints missing file and chunk counts as placeholders instead of omitting them.

constraints.notes: This GitHub-derived reproduction is expected to be repaired in code, not by changing tests.

notes: Code-only localized reproduction of openclaw/openclaw issue #62855.
